species: To assess the role of GFI1 in the DDR, we exploited an established Gfi1 knock-out mouse model.
cell_type: GFI1 is required in T cells for the regulation of key DNA damage signaling and repair proteins.
disease_state: GFI1 is a transcriptional regulator expressed in lymphoid cells, and an “oncorequisite” factor required for development and maintenance of T-lymphoid leukemia.
sample_source: To assess the role of GFI1 in the DDR, we exploited an established Gfi1 knock-out mouse model.
cell_line: we used a previously described Gfi1 knock-in (KI) strain expressing the human GFI1 gene in place of the murine counterpart, and a knockdown (KD) strain expressing reduced levels of the “knocked in” human GFI1.
strain: Mice have been bred on to C57BL/6 genetic background...
